text.title: error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for error-handling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This UUID is not correlated with any log line, span, or Sentry event. It creates a false sense of traceability.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SQLAlchemy exceptions include the full query, table name, and constraint — all internal. Return a generic message and log the exception server-side with the traceId.

failure-mode.description: Adding ProblemDetail only to 422 (validation) and omitting 401, 403, 404, 409, 500.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every error status a client can receive needs the same envelope. Clients must not branch on Content-Type to detect whether an error is RFC 7807 or ad-hoc.

failure-mode.description: Reusing a Python RFC 7807 pattern verbatim in Go or Java.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each language has its own idioms: errors.As in Go, @ControllerAdvice in Spring, ProblemDetail.forRawStatusCode in Spring 6. Require per-language idiomatic implementations.
